# EEGLAB

> EEGLAB is an open-source MATLAB toolbox from the Swartz Center for Computational Neuroscience
> (SCCN) for processing continuous and event-related EEG, MEG, and other electrophysiological
> data: filtering, artifact rejection, independent component analysis (ICA), time-frequency
> analysis, and group study. It has a graphical interface and a scripting interface.

## Start here
- [About EEGLAB](https://eeglab.org): what it is and what it does
- [Tutorials](https://eeglab.org/tutorials): step-by-step guides
- [Download and install](https://eeglab.org/download): get EEGLAB
- [Source code](https://github.com/sccn/eeglab): the MATLAB toolbox

## Use it
- [Scripting](https://eeglab.org/tutorials): run EEGLAB from scripts, headless with `eeglab nogui`
- Function manifest: a machine-readable catalog of the core functions accompanies this site (function-manifest.json)
- Data model: datasets are the EEG structure, stored as `.set` (metadata) plus `.fdt` (data); validate with `eeg_checkset`

## In the ecosystem
- [BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io): organize datasets (the `bids-matlab-tools` plugin imports and exports BIDS)
- [HED](https://www.hedtags.org): annotate events
- [NEMAR](https://nemar.org) and [OpenNeuro](https://openneuro.org): datasets to analyze

## Notes for agents
- EEGLAB is MATLAB; there is no package registry to enumerate. Use the function manifest, not guesswork.
- Prefer the `pop_` functions; always run `eeg_checkset(EEG)` after modifying a dataset.
